claimText: Under the Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Amendment Rules, 2025, Compensatory Afforestation (CA) land can be transferred to the Forest Department or notified as a protected area under the Indian Forest Act, 1927.
evidenceExcerpt: Compensatory Afforestation (CA) land can now be transferred to the Forest Department or notified as a protected area under the Indian Forest Act, 1927.
examUse: Statement-based question on the new provisions regarding the utilization and status of Compensatory Afforestation land.
distractorHooks: CA land must be transferred to the Revenue Department
CA land cannot be notified as a protected area
CA land is auctioned to private entities for eco-tourism

claimText: The Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Amendment Rules, 2025 mandate that mining of critical or strategic minerals requires Compensatory Afforestation on degraded forest land at a minimum of double the extent.
evidenceExcerpt: Mining of critical/strategic minerals requires CA on degraded forest land at a minimum of double the extent.
examUse: Statement-based question on the specific environmental compensation requirements for mining critical and strategic minerals.
distractorHooks: CA is required at an equal extent for critical minerals
Mining of critical minerals is completely exempted from Compensatory Afforestation
CA for critical minerals must be done exclusively on non-forest land

claimText: State governments have the authority to grant initial 'working permission' for linear projects such as roadworks, railway tracks, and transmission lines under the Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Amendment Rules, 2025.
evidenceExcerpt: State governments are allowed to grant initial 'working permission' for linear projects like roadworks, railway tracks, and transmission lines.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the delegation of powers between the Centre and States for infrastructure projects in forest areas.
distractorHooks: Only the Central Government (MoEFCC) can grant initial working permission for linear projects
Gram Sabhas are the sole authority to grant initial working permission for linear projects
State governments can grant initial working permission for all types of mining projects
